title: Super Group (SGHC) Limited (SGHC) Stock Slides as Market Rises: Facts to Know Before You Trade
content:
Super Group (SGHC) Limited (SGHC) closed the most recent trading day at $10.58, moving -3.2% from the previous trading session. The stock trailed the S&P 500, which registered a daily gain of 0.62%. On the other hand, the Dow registered a gain of 0.58%, and the technology-centric Nasdaq increased by 0.83%.

Shares of the company witnessed a gain of 0.55% over the previous month, beating the performance of the Consumer Discretionary sector with its loss of 1.73%, and underperforming the S&P 500's gain of 0.8%.

Investors will be eagerly watching for the performance of Super Group (SGHC) Limited in its upcoming earnings disclosure. The company's upcoming EPS is projected at $0.17, signifying a 41.67% increase compared to the same quarter of the previous year. Meanwhile, the Zacks Consensus Estimate for revenue is projecting net sales of $603 million, up 16.63% from the year-ago period.

In terms of the entire fiscal year, the Zacks Consensus Estimates predict earnings of $0.8 per share and a revenue of $2.56 billion, indicating changes of 0% and +39.45%, respectively, from the former year.

Additionally, investors should keep an eye on any recent revisions to analyst forecasts for Super Group (SGHC) Limited. These revisions typically reflect the latest short-term business trends, which can change frequently. As a result, we can interpret positive estimate revisions as a good sign for the business outlook.

Our research reveals that these estimate alterations are directly linked with the stock price performance in the near future. To utilize this, we have created the Zacks Rank, a proprietary model that integrates these estimate changes and provides a functional rating system.

The Zacks Rank system, spanning from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), boasts an impressive track record of outperformance, audited externally, with #1 ranked stocks yielding an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Over the past month, there's been no change in the Zacks Consensus EPS estimate. Super Group (SGHC) Limited currently has a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy).

Looking at its valuation, Super Group (SGHC) Limited is holding a Forward P/E ratio of 13.66. This denotes a discount relative to the industry average Forward P/E of 16.9.

The Gaming industry is part of the Consumer Discretionary sector. This industry, currently bearing a Zacks Industry Rank of 143, finds itself in the bottom 42% echelons of all 250+ industries.

The Zacks Industry Rank gauges the strength of our individual industry groups by measuring the average Zacks Rank of the individual stocks within the groups. Our research shows that the top 50% rated industries outperform the bottom half by a factor of 2 to 1.

title: How The Super Group (SGHC) Story Is Shifting After FY25 Beat And 2026 Uncertainty
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

The latest analyst update on Super Group (SGHC) centers on a price target set at $18 per share, compared with a model fair value of $17.38 that remains unchanged. Bullish and bearish voices are split on how much this new target reflects stronger perceived execution after FY25 versus reliance on results exceeding baseline expectations in 2026. Read on to see how these competing views shape the evolving narrative around the shares and what to watch as new information comes through.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for Super Group (SGHC) shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Super Group (SGHC).

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Benchmark lifted its price target on Super Group to $18 from $17 after FY25 results came in ahead of what it expected, which signals increased confidence in how the business is executing against its current plan. The firm talks about "a pathway for results to exceed baseline expectations as 2026 progresses," so its case for upside is linked to the potential for stronger financial delivery over time rather than only near term factors. Benchmark maintains a positive rating alongside the higher target, which shows that its favorable stance is tied to both the updated valuation work and its view of the company’s ability to build on recent performance.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Benchmark’s thesis still depends on results moving beyond its baseline expectations in 2026, which leaves room for disappointment if execution or external conditions do not support that pathway.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:SGHC 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for Super Group (SGHC). See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Spruce Point Capital Management issued a report titled "A Super Simple Short Thesis" with a "Strong Sell" opinion on Super Group, citing risk of market underperformance and estimating long term downside of about 20% to 50% to roughly $4.25 to $6.80 per share. Super Group announced an annual dividend program target of at least $0.20 cash per share in 2026, to be paid quarterly subject to Board approval and other uses of funds, and declared a first 2026 dividend of $0.05 per share payable on March 31, 2026 to shareholders of record on March 16, 2026. The Board declared a special cash dividend of $0.25 per ordinary share, payable on February 9, 2026 to shareholders of record as of February 2, 2026. Super Group issued earnings guidance, projecting 2026 total revenue greater than $2.55b and reiterating 2025 revenue expectations within the previously stated range of $2.17b to $2.27b.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Super Group (SGHC)

Fair value stays at $17.38 per share, unchanged from the prior estimate. Assumed long term revenue growth remains at 7.54% with no material revision. Target net profit margin is held at 18.82% with only a minor refinement in the calculation. Assumed future P/E multiple is stable at 20.48x with a very small downward tweak. The discount rate is essentially unchanged at 8.22%, with only a very small downward adjustment.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives connect a company’s business story to analysts’ forecasts and an estimated fair value, so you can see how the numbers tie back to real world developments. They refresh as new data, guidance, and risks come through, keeping the thesis up to date.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Super Group (SGHC) to stay up to date on:

How geographic expansion and supportive regulation in core regions such as Africa and Europe are shaping Super Group’s addressable market and user growth. The role of technology investment, product features like Bet Builder and micro betting, and brand sponsorships in supporting user retention and unit economics. Key risks around tighter regulation, intense competition that can lift marketing spend, and heavy tech investment that may not always translate into higher profitability.

title: Does Super Group (SGHC) Offer Value After Strong Three Year Share Price Gain?
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

If you are wondering whether Super Group (SGHC) at around US$10.63 offers fair value or a potential mispricing, the key is to look past the headline share price and into what that price implies. The stock has posted a 1.9% return over the last 7 days, while the 30 day and year to date returns are 5.2% and 8.7% declines, set against a 1 year return of 85.1% and a very large 3 year gain of 215.1%. These moves have kept Super Group on the radar of investors looking for online betting and gaming exposure, with ongoing coverage focusing on how its operations support the current share price. Much of the recent commentary circles back to whether the current valuation lines up with the business fundamentals rather than short term trading activity. On Simply Wall St’s 6 point valuation checklist, Super Group scores 5 out of 6. This sets up a closer look at how traditional models like DCF and multiples compare, and points to an even broader way to think about valuation that will be covered at the end of this article.

Super Group (SGHC) delivered 85.1% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Hospitality industry.

Approach 1: Super Group (SGHC) Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of future cash flows and discounts them back to today, aiming to translate those projections into a single present value per share.

For Super Group, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is reported at about $263.6 million. Simply Wall St then projects future Free Cash Flow, using analyst inputs where available and extending them over a ten year horizon. For example, the 2027 Free Cash Flow is projected at $535.2 million, and the 2035 estimate in the model is $831.2 million, with each year discounted back to today.

Pulling these discounted cash flows together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $24.16 per share. Against a current share price around $10.63, this implies an intrinsic discount of roughly 56.0%, which in this model indicates that the shares are trading well below the DCF estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Super Group (SGHC) is undervalued by 56.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 59 more high quality undervalued stocks.SGHC Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Super Group (SGHC).

Story Continues

Approach 2: Super Group (SGHC) Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay per share to the earnings the business generates. It gives you a quick sense of how many years of current earnings the market is pricing in, which is especially relevant when earnings are a key driver of investor attention.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends heavily on growth expectations and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower one.

Super Group currently trades on a P/E of about 23.30x, compared with the Hospitality industry average of roughly 21.31x and a peer group average of 37.20x. Simply Wall St’s proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Super Group is 27.26x. This Fair Ratio estimates the P/E that might be appropriate given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks, rather than relying only on broad peer or industry comparisons.

With the current P/E of 23.30x sitting below the Fair Ratio of 27.26x, the P/E based view points to the shares trading at a discount to that model implied level.

Result: UNDERVALUEDNYSE:SGHC P/E Ratio as at Apr 2026

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Super Group (SGHC) Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation, and on Simply Wall St that starts with Narratives, where you set out your story for Super Group by linking your assumptions about future revenue, earnings and margins to a financial forecast, which then feeds into a fair value that you can compare with the current share price.

Each Narrative on the Community page is a simple, structured view that combines this story and forecast into a fair value range, making it easier to decide whether Super Group looks attractive, fully priced or expensive based on your own assumptions rather than anyone else’s.

Because Narratives on the platform are updated automatically when fresh information comes through, such as new earnings guidance or reports like the recent “Super Simple Short Thesis,” you can see in one place how bullish and bearish views translate into different fair values.

For example, one Super Group Narrative might lean closer to the more optimistic analyst targets around US$20.00, while another might sit nearer the short thesis range of about US$4.25 to US$6.80, giving you a clear sense of how different outlooks on revenue guidance, margins and risk translate into very different views of what the shares are worth today.

title: Super Group (SGHC) Limited (SGHC) Stock Falls Amid Market Uptick: What Investors Need to Know
content:
In the latest trading session, Super Group (SGHC) Limited (SGHC) closed at $10.63, marking a -2.57% move from the previous day. This move lagged the S&P 500's daily gain of 0.11%. On the other hand, the Dow registered a loss of 0.13%, and the technology-centric Nasdaq increased by 0.18%.

Prior to today's trading, shares of the company had lost 5.7% was narrower than the Consumer Discretionary sector's loss of 6.69% and lagged the S&P 500's loss of 4.28%.

The investment community will be closely monitoring the performance of Super Group (SGHC) Limited in its forthcoming earnings report. The company's upcoming EPS is projected at $0.17, signifying a 41.67% increase compared to the same quarter of the previous year. Meanwhile, the latest consensus estimate predicts the revenue to be $603 million, indicating a 16.63% increase compared to the same quarter of the previous year.

SGHC's full-year Zacks Consensus Estimates are calling for earnings of $0.8 per share and revenue of $2.56 billion. These results would represent year-over-year changes of 0% and +39.45%, respectively.

It's also important for investors to be aware of any recent modifications to analyst estimates for Super Group (SGHC) Limited. These revisions help to show the ever-changing nature of near-term business trends. Hence, positive alterations in estimates signify analyst optimism regarding the business and profitability.

Based on our research, we believe these estimate revisions are directly related to near-term stock moves. To take advantage of this, we've established the Zacks Rank, an exclusive model that considers these estimated changes and delivers an operational rating system.

The Zacks Rank system, stretching from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), has a noteworthy track record of outperforming, validated by third-party audits, with stocks rated #1 producing an average annual return of +25% since the year 1988. From a valuation perspective, Super Group (SGHC) Limited is currently exchanging hands at a Forward P/E ratio of 13.64. This represents a discount compared to its industry average Forward P/E of 16.22.
